sections.paragraph:
ἀνατάσσομαι 1 aor. ἀνεταξάμην lit. ‘arrange in proper order’; fig., to organize in a series (s. τάσσω; Plut., Mor. 968c; Iren. 3, 21, 2 [Harv. II 114, 12]) διήγησιν ἀ. draw up, compose/compile a narrative Lk 1:1, implying use of traditional material and, as the context indicates, with emphasis on orderly sequence (synon. συντάσσεσθαι ‘compile’; cp. Syr., Copt., Goth. versions; Athanasius’ 39th Festival Letter: EPreuschen, Analecta2 II 1910, p. 43, 9; so Hippiatr. 1, 1 in a prologue reminiscent of Lk 1:1; EpArist 144). Cp. PCorssen, GGA 1899, 317f; Zahn on Lk 1:1; PScheller, De hellenist. conscribendae historiae arte, diss. Leipz. 1911, 23; JMansion, Serta Leodiensia 1930, 261–67; HCadbury, JBL 52, ’33, 56–58; LAlexander, The Preface to Luke’s Gospel ’93, 110f. S. also on παρακολουθέω 4.—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

synon. synon. = synonym, synonymous

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

Copt. Copt. = Coptic

Athanasius Athanasius of Alexandria, IV a.d.—List 5

Hippiatr. Hippiatr. = Corpus hippiatricorum Graecorum, IX a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

GGA GGA = Göttingische Gelehrte Anzeigen—List 6

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

diss. diss. = dissertation

Leipz. Leipz. = Leipzig

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 73
